item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Then I’ll see you guys later,” Yuan said his goodbyes to the others before following Elder Sun to the Inner Court examination area, which was held not too far away from the Hunting Grounds. When they arrived, Yuan noticed there were about 30 other disciples there. “Did you think you would be the only one taking the examination?” Elder Sun asked. “Of course not.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Your black ribbon?” Yuan turned to face the disciple with amusement. “Y-Yeah! If I hadn’t distracted that magical beast, you wouldn’t have been able to defeat it!” the disciple blurted out, his voice slightly unsteady. Despite his confident-sounding words, there was no conviction in his tone. It was clear—even to himself—that he didn’t truly believe what he was saying. “What an amusing thing to say. Alright. If you truly think you deserve this black ribbon, come get it from me.” Yuan extended the ribbon toward the disciple.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After a day of rest, Yuan entered the Inner Court with Elder Sun’s medallion to look for a new house. Of course, he already had one in mind. Once he found a servant to assist him, Yuan went straight to Tian Yang’s living quarters and asked about its availability. The servant retrieved a jade slip and took a moment to look through its content before responding, “Sorry, but this place is already taken by another disciple.” Yuan narrowed his eyes upon hearing this and asked, “Is this disciple’s name Tian Yang?”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Alright, show me your progress with the Peerless Immortal Technique,” Elder Sun said to Yuan. Yuan nodded and showed off his progress, which had reached the third level. ‘To think he’d enter the third level of the technique after just a year of training… As I thought, his talents when it comes to comprehension are monstrous.’ Elder Sun thought to herself. “Okay, now show me your other—”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “I know you can create whatever rules you want, but isn’t 10,000,000 points asking for too much? One can get several powerful treasures with that many points.” Yuan tried to convince the Sect Leader to be reasonable. However, the Sect Leader remained firm on his price and said, “Ten million points. I won’t accept a single point less.” Yuan then said, “How about this? I am willing to pay 10,000,000 points, but can I pay you later? I want to follow Elder Sun to the Desolate Continent, but I need at least half a year to collect that many points.” The Sect Leader did not immediately respond. After a moment of silence, he finally spoke, “Since I know you’re more than capable of collecting that many points, I’m willing to delay your payment for one year. However, for every year you fail to pay me, I will add another ten million points to your debt. If you fail to pay me back within 4 years, I will disqualify you from the trial. What do you think?”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Elder Sun, I am serious. I want to go with you to the Desolate Continent. You have no idea how much I sacrificed to get the Sect Leader’s approval,” Yuan said with a serious expression on his face. Elder Sun sighed, “Whatever. You’ll go by yourself if I refuse anyway, so I might as well bring you with me. You’ll be a little safer that way. Promise me you’ll not leave my side for as long as we’re there.” “I promise,” Yuan nodded. “Come to the Immortal Sun Peak in three days. We’ll leave then,” she said a moment later.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Elder Sun, it’s unfair that I’m the only one answering your questions. Let me ask you a few questions, as well,” Yuan eventually said. Elder Sun narrowed her eyes, clearly hesitant to have her background questioned. ‘Since he is my first disciple…’ A moment later, she nodded, “Alright, you can ask me a few questions. However, you can’t ask anything too personal.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: As they traveled further away from the Flying Sword Sect, Xiao Hua thought to herself, 'It's only been a few days since Brother Yuan became a Cultivator, yet he is already powerful enough to hold his own against Spirit Warriors, even though he's only a Ninth Level Spirit Apprentice. This kind of growth is unheard of and is terrifying, to say the least. His experience with the sword is also increasing at a frightening rate, almost like he's the reincarnation of a Sword Emperor.' "Besides wandering around aimlessly for valuable resources and collecting monster cores, there is really nothing else to do." "Additionally, because Brother Yuan is already a Ninth Level Spirit Apprentice, he has to attain enlightenment before he can make a breakthrough and become a Spirit Warrior." Xiao Hua turned to look at Yuan and spoke, "Brother Yuan, what would you like to do? Even if you keep consuming monster cores to fill your Qi, once you reach the peak of the Spirit Apprentice realm, you will not be a

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: "What is this instrument called?" Yuan asked after seeing the instrument that resembled a guitar. "This instrument is called a pipa," Fei Yuyan quickly responded. 'A pipa, huh… I wonder if it functions similar to the guitar…' Yuan wondered to himself as they wandered around the room, admiring the beautifully crafted instruments inside the display case. After spending a couple of minutes in the pipa room, they went to another room.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Yuan and the other onlookers stared at Elder Sun with wide eyes. “What a powerful treasure!” “Isn’t she the one with the lion flying treasure? You must be incredibly stupid to try and rob someone as rich as that…” The onlookers shook their heads at the bandits, mocking them in silence.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: ‘Huang Xiao Li? What is she doing here? No… more importantly, why is her family alive?!’ Yuan stared at Huang Xiao Li as she chatted with her family, a bright smile on her face. “Xiao Yang? What’s the matter? Why did you suddenly stop moving? Do you see a bandit again?” Elder Sun asked when she noticed his weird behavior. “Please give me a moment.” Without elaborating further, Yuan left Elder Sun’s side and approached Huang Xiao Li and her family.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: ‘Shit… what the hell is happening? This world is actually real, and so are the people in it? If triggering Huang Xiao Li’s memories of Tian Yang caused that world-ending phenomenon, why are Elder Sun and the other disciples fine?’ Yuan pondered as he rode on the flying treasure with Elder Sun. Although the Sect Leader didn’t go into details, he’d actually revealed a lot through his speech, most of them being subtle hints. ‘At first, neither Huang Xiao Li nor her family recognized me, and it didn’t seem like they were pretending, either. However, shortly after seeing me, they regained their memories of me. From this, I can assume that the people of this world don’t know Tian Yang until they meet him, which triggers memories of him.’ ‘This begs the question… why is this happening? Why did they not have memories of Tian Yang until they saw me? Are their memories of Tian Yang being suppressed? For what reason?’

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “What the fuck are you doing, you crazy bastard!” When Yuan tossed the monster core into his mouth, Elder Sun reacted instinctively. In a flash, her hand shot out, seizing him by the throat with a firm grip. With her other hand, she forced her fingers into his mouth, desperately trying to retrieve the monster core before it could be swallowed. “?!” Yuan was taken aback when Elder Sun suddenly shoved her fingers into his mouth, but it was already too late—the monster core had long since melted and been absorbed. All she managed to do was wiggle her slender fingers around awkwardly, accomplishing nothing but an increasingly uncomfortable situation for both of them.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “How long do you plan on training here?” Elder Sun asked Yuan when he continued shortly after the Immortal Ensnaring Vault was activated. “Since we can’t leave, I will train until the Immortal Ensnaring Vault is deactivated. I’m sure the Sect Leader won’t punish us for returning late, as we have no control over our situation.” Elder Sun nodded and said, “Alright, but if the Immortal Ensnaring Vault isn’t gone by the end of the year, I’ll take us out of here, even if it might inconvenience the Nine Immortal Clans.” Thus, Yuan continued his training in the Desolate Continent.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Sometime later, the clerk returned with the spatial rings and said, “I can give you 72,283,000 points for all of the materials here.” Yuan smiled and nodded, “Sold.” The results were much better than he’d anticipated, but considering that he’d also hunted Spirit Enlightenment magical beasts, he wasn’t too surprised. After selling all of the material he’d collected from 8 months of grinding, Yuan could easily repay his debt. However, he didn’t immediately visit the Sect Leader and asked the clerk, “Can I purchase contribution points for the sect?”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Shortly after arriving at Ji Ran’s living quarters, Yuan quickly realized that he wasn’t home. Instead of blindly wandering around to look for the others, Yuan asked a servant for the location of their living quarters. However, even after checking all of them, none of them were available. “They must be quite busy after becoming Inner Court disciples.” After pondering for a moment, Yuan decided to just take the Core Disciple examination and get it out of the way.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “It is exactly as it sounds. This duplicated world is an exact replica of the original—from the people to the locations, even down to the timeline. At least, that’s how it should have been,” the Sect Leader said, his voice trailing off as he paused. “Tian Yang,” Yuan spoke. “As far as I am aware, he doesn’t exist in this world. Elder Sun tried looking for him in the Desolate Continent but to no avail. However, for some reason, her memories progress as if he exists.” The Sect Leader nodded, “Indeed. Although Tian Yang couldn’t be replicated for some reason, the people who had a connection to Tian Yang in the original world were perfectly duplicated, their memories included. However, that memory would only trigger if they are reminded of Tian Yang.” “Why couldn’t Tian Yang be duplicated? He’s only a single individual.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Don’t worry, I will tell you everything I know about Outer Gods. It won’t be much, so don’t have any high expectations,” Yuan said. After taking a deep breath, he continued, “I know of two other Outer Gods, but I only know one of their names. Rashara.” Of course, Yuan knew Shura’s name, but he decided not to reveal it. “Rashara? The name is quite similar to the Outer God I know—Rashira. Do you think they’re related?” The Sect Leader remarked.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “What the hell? Who was that?” The disciple who was with Meng Lili asked shortly after Yuan left. Meng Lili shook her head and said, “I don’t know, but he looked familiar, and he appeared to know me, too.” “He was probably just another admirer. You’re a Core Disciple, so it’s not weird.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Sometime later, Yuan followed Fei Yuyan and approached the guards with her. "Welcome, Fairy Fei." The guards immediately recognized her beautiful appearance. "It's been a while, you two. I would like to go inside with a friend today," Fei Yuyan said to them. The guards turned to look at Yuan's masked face, but since he was wearing the Dragon Essence Temple's uniforms, they didn't bother to question his identity.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Is there anything I can say that will change your mind?” Elder Sun asked, still reluctant to let him visit her family. Yuan smiled. “You should know me well enough by now to know the answer to that.” Elder Sun sighed, “Even if your life might be endangered?”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Looks like everyone is here,” Yuan said after Tan Songyun, Xi Meili, and Yingzi returned to the City of Radiance. “Young Master, you’re back!” Feng Yuxiang emerged from his dantian a moment later and immediately embraced him. “Feng Feng? What happened to you after I entered the Immortal Monastery?” Yuan asked her.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “I understand their desire for power, but what’s the point if you turn into a monster at the end?” Xi Meili shook her head after hearing about Demon Worshippers. She could still vividly recall the grotesque transformation she witnessed during Yuan’s trial. “That usually only happens when one consumes too much demon blood. A few drops won’t turn one into a monster, but there is still a high chance it will affect their mind by making them more aggressive, hot-tempered, and unreasonable,” Yuan explained further. “I was told this place was attacked recently by the Demon Worshippers, hence why it’s empty right now.” Tan Songyun suddenly revealed. She turned to look at Yuan and asked, “So, what are your plans now?”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Although the Extreme Yin Lake was located beside the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect and primarily visited by its disciples, it was classified as a natural training ground rather than exclusive sect property. As such, outsiders were allowed to enter freely. However, despite this openness, the lake’s unforgiving cold served as a natural barrier. The chilling Yin energy was so intense that only those who had cultivated the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect’s unique techniques could withstand it for extended periods. For anyone else, this training ground was a perilous area. Because of this, while technically open to all, in practice, the Extreme Yin Lake remained a place almost exclusively occupied by the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect’s disciples, and even then, only their most talented and experienced disciples would dare to train there. When Yuan reached about 300 meters from the Extreme Yin Lake, he came to a stop. The air around him was so frigid that even the moisture in his breath froze instantly

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Missing… so you think your disciples are being kidnapped by the Demon Worshippers?” Tan Songyun asked after hearing this. “That, or they were killed,” sighed the Sect Leader. “What is the sect doing about this?” “So far, we’ve recalled most of our disciples back to the sect, unless they’re on a really important mission. We have also restricted their movements to within the sect’s boundary. Other than that… I don’t know what else can be done.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Shortly after greeting each other, Sect Leader Yun led Sun Ling Cai to her headquarters. Xi Meili and Tan Songyun followed them after receiving the Sect Leader’s permission. “Am I the only Demon Sealer here? What about the Demon Sealing Grotto?” “You’re the only Demon Sealer here.” “I see. I was half-expecting them to be here, too.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: The Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect’s disciples clashed with the Demon Worshippers. Due to the close distance between their sect and the Extreme Yin Lake, they only needed to defend themselves for several minutes before help arrived. However, the disciples quickly realized that lasting even a minute would be challenging. The Demon Worshippers were incredibly powerful and fought without any regard for their own safety. Moreover, despite having lower cultivation than the disciples, the Demon Worshippers displayed prowess that surpassed the disciples. This abnormal power is one of many reasons why Demon Worshippers abandon their humanity. “I won’t be able to endure this much longer, Senior Sister!”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Although Sun Ling Cai wanted to intervene and help the masked individual, her curiosity got the best of her, and she decided to watch him a little longer. As for Sect Leader Yun, she couldn’t care less and immediately went to save her disciples the moment she noticed them. “Sect Leader! You’re here!” The disciples cried tears of joy when they saw her beautiful face.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Senior Sister Yan’s situation, huh?” An awkward expression appeared on Sun Ling Cai’s face. “It’s a bit complicated.” Seeing Sun Ling Cai’s expression, Yuan frowned and asked, “Did something bad happen to her?” “What? Oh, it’s nothing like that, but… it might take some time to explain, so let’s continue this chat later.” Sun Ling Cai’s gaze shifted to the sealed Demon Worshipper. Yuan realized what she was trying to say and nodded, “You’re right. Let’s continue this conversation after we deal with the Demon Worshippers. I was going to interrogate them, but if you have other plans, please let me know.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “You’re a Master Demon Sealer. I don’t qualify to worry about you,” Yuan chuckled. “That’s not true. Your Demon Sealing Aura is powerful enough to qualify as a Master Demon Sealer—if you aren’t one already.” “I’m definitely not a Master Demon Sealer.” “If you say so…”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Ting~ Ting~ Ting~ This unknown beauty continued to play the zither despite Yuan's sudden appearance, and Yuan stood there silently to listen to her music, feeling as though every music note was pulling his heartstrings alongside something he couldn't really pinpoint— his soul. After an unknown amount of time has passed, the beauty finally stopped playing the music before placing her hands on her lap and turning to look at Yuan with her beautiful eyes that resembled jewels. "What do you think of my music?" she suddenly asked him, her clear voice sounding as beautiful as the zither music if not even more pleasant sounding.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “This is my first time hearing about such a demon. Can you tell me more about it?” Yuan asked. Sun Ling Cai nodded and said, “Then let me tell you what the Nine Heavens Law and Order told us. They claimed this demon was only at the Spirit King level when they first encountered it. Yet, this demon was able to overpower several immortals who have restricted their cultivation to the Divine King realm.” “The demon wielded two Soul Weapons, a spear and a sword. They even claimed the demon was able to use Enhanced Sword Aura. They also claimed that this demon was able to regenerate from complete annihilation and even had the aura of a beast. Ridiculous, isn’t it? Who would believe that such a demon would exist?” It was common knowledge that demons don’t use weapons, let alone Soul Weapons, and if they did, these weapons would be crafted from their own blood. Moreover, a demon using Sword Aura was unheard of, much less Enhanced Sword Aura.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: The moment the Demon Worshippers retrieved their weapons, Yuan wasted no time attacking them. Coincidentally, the Demon Worshippers were also wielding swords, and they were bewildered when they failed to activate their swords’ ability to block Yuan’s attack, almost as if an unseen force had sealed their swords. “Wha—?!” “My sword?!” Yuan took advantage of their brief moment of shock and landed two swift and seemingly non-lethal strikes.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After sensing the overwhelming malicious presence, Sun Ling Cai had to force herself to turn around. Every instinct in her body warned her to run, yet she stood her ground. At first, she saw nothing—just the empty, distorted air ahead, heavy with oppressive energy. But then, a silhouette began to take shape, emerging slowly from the shadows as if reality itself were bending to make way for it. Each step it took was deliberate, and with every step, the pressure grew heavier, colder—like a noose tightening around her soul. ‘A second demon? How is that possible?! My divine sense has been active and surrounding this place ever since we entered, yet I only just sensed it! Did it teleport in here just now?’ Sun Ling Cai couldn’t fathom how she could’ve missed this demon, who was emitting a ridiculous amount of demonic energy.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After sealing the Demon General, Yuan said to the captives trembling in fear behind him, “You can open your eyes again. I have dealt with the demon.” When the captives opened their eyes, their faces became filled with shock after seeing the sealed demon. After all, only a few seconds had passed since they were told to close their eyes. ‘He sealed a Demon General in seconds?! How is that even possible?!’ The captives couldn’t comprehend it.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Sun Ling Cai stared at Yuan’s face, stunned. It was identical to the statue enshrined in the Demon Sealing Clan’s Grand Library—handsome, timeless, and unmistakable. It had been years since Sun Ling Cai last saw Yuan, and in that time, he had matured. He no longer merely resembled the Divine Paragon—he was his exact likeness, down to the last detail. “F-Founder…? That’s impossible…” Sun Ling Cai had spent many years gazing at the Divine Paragon’s statue since she joined the Demon Sealing Clan, and as a painter who painted countless pieces of him, there was no way she could mistake his features. Yet even now, she struggled to believe what her eyes were showing her.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Did you know? The Demon Sealing Sword was once an ordinary sword that evolved through the demon blood it was constantly soaked in.” Yuan calmly revealed this information as he looked at the sword in his grasp. If it hadn’t been protected by his Demon Sealing Aura, the Demon Sealing Sword would’ve most likely evolved into a cursed treasure instead. ‘I need to run!’ At the sight of the Demon Sealing Sword, the Demon Emperor abandoned all thoughts of battle. Fear took hold, and it began desperately searching for a way to escape.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: ‘That level of demonic energy… another Demon Emperor has appeared?! What is happening?!’ Sun Ling Cai’s jaw dropped after seeing Demon Emperor Malice’s sudden appearance. It was so shocking that it forced her to stop painting briefly. “You probably already know this, but in case you don’t, ‘Malice’ was the first Demon Emperor to be slain by me. Although Malice wasn’t the strongest amongst the Demon Emperors, he came the closest to defeating me—mostly because I was still inexperienced back then.” Yuan looked back at their battle as his gaze stared at Demon Emperor Malice, who stood there with a blank look on its face, resembling a soulless puppet. He gave a simple gesture, beckoning the figure forward. In response, Demon Emperor Malice stepped toward him—and without hesitation, dropped to its knees, then lowered itself further until it was bowing low, its forearms to the ground in a show of absolute submission.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “By the way, why are you hiding your identity, even going as far as to wear a mask?” Sun Ling Cai asked Yuan when he wore his mask again. “Do you remember that demon the Nine Heavens Law and Order warned you about?” “That ridiculous and unbelievable existence? What about it?” “Well, that ‘demon’ is me,” Yuan calmly revealed.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Is that why you were at the Extreme Yin Lake? To improve your Cold Immunity?” Sun Ling Cai asked. “That’s right. I need to train my body in a place with extremely strong Yin Qi.” Sun Ling Cai pondered for a moment before speaking, “Actually, I might be able to help you in this regard.” She retrieved a scrolled painting from her spatial ring and continued, “This is one of my masterpieces. It’s called ‘The Cold That Cuts Heaven’, and I painted it when I visited the White Hell, one of four forbidden zones within the Nine Heavens.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Yuan stared at the new notifications with raised eyebrows. He didn’t know the Celestial Emperor’s Curse had such an effect since it wasn’t mentioned in its description. <Heavenly Tribulation(Major) descends!> Before Yuan could even step outside or prepare himself, the Heavenly Tribulation struck without warning. The dark clouds above the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect roared with fury as a colossal bolt of lightning—large enough to engulf an entire building—crashed down, striking both Yuan and his house in a blinding explosion of light and power.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: "Zither Goddess's Ethernal Art, huh? Are you sure that you want to name it after me?" The Zither Goddess asked him afterward. Yuan nodded, "I'm sure." The Zither Goddess smiled, and then she said, "There is nothing left for me to teach you, and I hope you get to meet the real me outside one day. Perhaps you might be able to reignite my passion for the zither once again. Until then, Zither God." "Eh? What did you just call me?" Yuan's eyes widened with surprise when the Zither Goddess suddenly called him the 'Zither God', but alas, before he could get an answer, the scenery changed, and the young lady with otherworldly beauty disappeared from his sight.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Yuan shrugged and said, “It became more powerful when I provoked it.” “Y-You provoked the Heavenly Tribulation…? Not even a madman would dare to do something so insane…” Tan Songyun’s jaw dropped at this information. “Anyway…” Yuan turned to face Sect Leader Yun and bowed to her. “I apologize for summoning the Heavenly Tribulation right in the middle of your sect, even destroying a portion of it.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “I—I don’t know what you’re talking about!” Yan Hara exclaimed, slamming her hand on the desk as she shot to her feet. Her voice trembled, and the panic in her eyes was impossible to miss. Sun Ling Cai chuckled, “You’re really cute when you panic, Senior Sister Yan. You’re also very bad at acting. Who are you trying to fool with that kind of reaction? Anyway, I know that the Little Founder is the Divine Paragon’s reincarnation.” Yan Hara’s entire body trembled at such words. “H-How did you…?”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After the meeting, Sun Ling Cai and Yan Hara met up one more time before Sun Ling Cai made her way back to the Sixth Heaven. In the Sixth Heaven, before heading back to the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect to meet with Yuan, Sun Ling Cai dropped off the volunteer back at his home. “Wow, what happened here? Did a demon attack your sect while I was gone?” Sun Ling Cai was dumbfounded by the destruction in the sect and asked Sect Leader Yun. “No… A Supreme Heavenly Tribulation occurred when Xiao Yang attempted to breakthrough to the Divine Realm.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “How did it go? I hope you’ve achieved satisfactory results,” Sun Ling Cai said. “How long have I been inside the painting?” Yuan asked. “A little over three months.” “I see… It wasn’t too bad. However, if I want to advance further, I’d need to visit the real White Hell personally.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “I’m trying to learn—complete my Immortal Qi.” Yuan showed Yan Hara his incomplete Immortal Qi. “You can already use Immortal Qi? But you’re only at—Divine King?! How did you improve your cultivation so quickly?! You were only around Spirit King a few years ago!” Yan Hara just noticed his cultivation and was greatly bewildered by his progress. If she knew that he’d gone from Spirit Enlightenment to Divine King in the past month, who knows how she’d react. As for Sun Ling Cai, her mouth and eyes were wide open. She knew Yuan was only Spirit Enlightenment last month, and she knew he’d triggered a Supreme Heavenly Tribulation to enter the Divine Realm, but she didn’t realize he was already Divine King.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Xi Meili stared at her cup of tea and hesitated to touch it, much less drink it. “What are you waiting for? Go ahead and drink it,” Yan Hara said right before downing her cup of tea in one motion. Sun Ling Cai also drank the tea without any hesitation. Xi Meili swallowed nervously before grabbing the cup. Then, with one swift and forceful movement, she drank it.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “When are you going to visit the Demon Sealing Grotto? I would love to go with you, but my position within the Demon Sealing Clan would only make things more difficult for you,” Yan Hara said. “I have some business at the Dragon Spiral Mountain, so I will be going there first. After that, I want to check up on my friends, but that won’t take long. If nothing else comes up, I’ll visit the Demon Sealing Grotto.” Yan Hara raised her eyebrows in a puzzled manner and inquired, “The Dragon Spiral Mountain? What business do you have with the Holy Dragon Clan? I thought they didn’t accept visitors.” “You’re aware that my current body is only an avatar of my soul, right?”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After entering the Sea of Dragons, it didn’t take long for the sea beasts to launch their assault on the Soaring Phoenix. Even though the flying treasure flew high above the waves, the creatures below unleashed powerful long-range attacks. Some even went as far as jumping out of the water to strike them using their entire body. The sea beasts of the Sea of Dragons were notoriously aggressive, attacking anything that moved without hesitation. This was one of many reasons why few dared to traverse its waters. Navigating the sea required not only specialized equipment but also a vast amount of resources to defend against the relentless onslaught of these powerful creatures. “Why are the sea creatures here so aggressive? It wasn’t nowhere this bad at the Purple Sea,” Xi Meili wondered out loud as she watched them bounce off an invisible barrier that protected the flying treasure. “Well, dragons are aggressive creatures by nature,” Feng Yuxiang said with a casual shrug.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After hearing Feng Yuxiang’s words, Yuan expanded the range of his divine sense. Although he kept it active at all times, he usually limited its scope to avoid distracting others—or worse, being mistaken for spying, a common source of conflict among cultivators. Several hundred miles away, Yuan’s divine sense locked onto a large gathering of individuals. They were unmistakably dragons—humanoid in form but marked by prominent horns and swaying dragon tails. There were around twenty of them, split into two distinct factions. One group was dressed in elegant white and gold robes, radiating a noble aura. The other wore black and light blue garments, their presence colder and more ominous. “The Holy Dragon Clan has monopolized the Dragon Spiral Mountain for far too long!” One of the black and blue-clothed individuals shouted angrily.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Well, if you’re not going to tell me where to find the Dragon Spiral Mountain, I’ll take my leave now.” Yuan said shortly after putting away the Dragon God’s Vengeance. Without giving the Holy Dragon Clan an opportunity to speak, Yuan turned around and flew away. “W-Wait a second—!” The Bronze Immortal from the Holy Dragon Clan tried to chase after Yuan but was blocked by the black-robed dragons.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: "Although it was only briefly, I have spoken to the young lady known as Fei Yuyan before. A really talented and respectful girl. I cannot imagine someone like that lying about such a thing. What about the person who can supposedly play the Soul Ensnaring Zither? Do you know anything about his background?" Senior Zou asked the two guards. "No, Master Zou. That person was wearing a black jade mask so we couldn't identify him. However, he was wearing the Dragon Essence Temple's disciple uniform, so we can only assume that he's a disciple there." Senior Zou nodded after a moment of silence, and then he said to the two guards, "Bring the Soul Ensnaring Zither here. I'll see for myself whether this mysterious individual can really play the zither once owned by the Zither Goddess!" Meanwhile, at the Royal Blossom Hotel, Elder Shan said to the three female disciples, "We'll see you all tomorrow morning."

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Identify yourselves!” A group of golden-robed dragons surrounded the Soaring Phoenix. “My name is Xiao Yang, and this is my traveling companion, Xi Meili.” “What purpose do you have at our Dragon Spiral Mountain?”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After entering the Dragon Spiral Mountain, the Dragon Emperor led Yuan to the mouth of a colossal cave, its entrance veiled in a faint, ancient pressure. As they approached, the Nine Dragon Symbols within Yuan stirred restlessly, trembling with excitement. Whatever it was resonating with was inside the cave. “This is the first time an outsider has visited this place,” said Holy Dragon Emperor as they reached the entrance. “It’s an honor,” Yuan smiled. “However, if you fail to prove your identity, you won’t be able to leave this place alive.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “W-What does this vixen think she’s doing to my Young Master?!” Feng Yuxiang exclaimed, her voice filled with envy as she watched this unfold from Yuan’s Dantian. The members of the Holy Dragon Clan stood frozen in place, eyes wide and jaws agape, as if the very ground had been pulled from beneath them. Some looked as though their souls had momentarily left their bodies, stunned beyond words by what they were witnessing. After what felt like an eternity, Dragon Goddess Yeyou finally released Yuan and spoke, “This doesn’t mean I have forgiven you, so don’t think for a second that you’re out of the hot water.” However, Yuan ignored her words and changed the topic, asking, “I didn’t know your soul wasn’t in one piece. Why didn’t you mention this before?”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Appeasing the Great One?” Yuan raised an eyebrow. The Dragon Emperor gave a solemn nod and began to explain, “Just as we are devoted to Dragon Goddess Yeyou, the Azure Dragon Clan offers their loyalty to the Great One. They would go to any lengths just to earn even a sliver of his attention.” “Unfortunately for them, the Great One rarely shows interest in anything, as he already possesses everything one could desire. However, there is one exception—he always shows interest in matters related to Dragon Goddess Yeyou. And while our Dragon Spiral Mountain may not hold any true connection to her… It’s enough to pique the Great One’s interest.” “Fortunately for us, the Great One is too prideful to personally take over this place, hence the Azure Dragon Clan’s motivation. Anyway, if we want to activate the teleport formation, we will need to retrieve the key from them, but they definitely won’t let it go without a fight.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: A few hours after Yuan and Xi Meili were given a living quarter, the Dragon Emperor showed up at their place. “I have contacted the Azure Dragon Clan, but it may take a few days, if not weeks, before we get a response,” he said. “In the meantime, you can treat the Dragon Spiral Mountain as if it’s your own home.” “I will do just that,” Yuan nodded. “Would you like to walk with me? I’ll give you a tour around the place. There’s also a lot I would still like to discuss with you.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “The Dragon Azure Clan! Did the Dragon Goddess send you here to get rid of those pests trying to steal our home?!” The young dragons’ eyes sparkled when Yuan mentioned the Dragon Azure Clan. “Who knows?” Yuan shrugged. A few of the young dragons then approached Xi Meili and asked her, “Are you also Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy?”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “There’s no way Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s envoy is only a Divine Emperor! You must be teasing me!” Sheng Ran refused to believe that would be Yuan’s true cultivation. Yuan smiled and said, “But it’s the truth.” “No way…” After a moment of awkward silence, Sheng Ran spoke again, “Okay… I’ll suppress my cultivation to match yours.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Don’t look at me like that. I’m not talking about myself.” The Dragon Emperor shook his head with a weak smile on his face. “Since you’ve only recently arrived in the Seventh Heaven, you probably haven’t heard of any rumors on the Dragon Emperors since they rarely get passed down to the lower heavens.” “There are only seven Dragon Emperors across the entire Nine Heavens—including myself,” the Dragon Emperor said solemnly. “Yet most of them are steeped in controversy. Take the Blood Dragon Emperor, for example. He is infamous for his uncontrollable bloodlust, going on murderous rampages every few years. Entire cities and bloodlines have been reduced to ruins beneath his wrath.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “The Great One, huh? Do you know his location?” Yuan asked. The Dragon Emperor shook his head and said, “The number of individuals who may know his location can be counted on one hand. He loves traveling the Nine Heavens in search for treasures and can be anywhere. When he’s not traveling, he’s most likely hidden within his own realm or slumbering somewhere in the Starry Sky.” “I see.” “Anyway, going back to the Azure Dragon Clan. They are one of the ten recognized royal bloodlines, but they are ranked last in terms of prowess and influence.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Of course, there will be others. This is a public training ground, after all,” said the middle-aged man. “That said, the majority of these individuals are from the dragon clans, so you won’t be seeing many humans.” “I see. Understandable.” “Whenever you’re ready to begin your training, simply pass through the gates behind me,” the middle-aged man instructed. Yuan nodded and, without hesitation, stepped through the gates, arriving at the first floor of the training area.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: 'What a weird dream…' Yuan thought to himself after waking up. However, for some reason, he felt a little bit sad that he'd woken up, almost like he wanted to continue dreaming and playing the zither with Zither Goddess. 'I wonder if I'll be able to play the zither with the real person if I go to the upper heavens…' Yuan sighed inwardly, feeling a strong desire to meet Zither Goddess. After breakfast, Yu Rou said, "Brother, the zither competition is tomorrow, right?"

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After being struck by the ice knight and being cursed with Glacial Trace, Yuan inspected his body. ‘My body definitely feels colder now, but it’s not enough.’ Since a single curse wasn’t enough to see any difference, Yuan decided to let the ice knight strike him a few more times. <You have been cursed with Glacial Trace>

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: An entire month has passed since Yuan accepted forty Glacial Traces on his body. Although his training progress has sped up tremendously, Yuan could tell that he would still not be able to achieve Cold Immunity within what little time he had left. Thus, he decided to push himself further and accepted another ten Glacial Traces, bringing his total to fifty. His recklessness was so mindboggling that it indirectly disturbed the others’ training. There was simply no way they could focus on their own training when there was such a madman in their presence. With only one week remaining, Yuan suddenly began making his way toward the massive ice structure at the center of the floor—the very source of the coldest, most concentrated energy in the entire training ground.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Are they soldiers?” Yuan asked, his gaze settling on the group of elite warriors stationed around the ship, standing like silent sentinels. “Yes,” the Dragon Emperor replied. “We’re not expecting a battle with the Azure Dragon Clan, but it’s a precaution. They’ll be doing the same on their end.” “I see.” Their journey to the Dragon Assembly Hall took just over a week, so they still had a few days to spare before their scheduled meeting with the Azure Dragon Clan.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Yuan continued, his voice steady and firm, “You may not know much about Dragon Goddess Yeyou—but surely, you should be aware that among the Three Dragons of Origin, she stands as the strongest. Whatever the Great One can offer, there’s little—if anything—that Dragon Goddess Yeyou cannot match or surpass.” Jiao Zhenhai fell silent to ponder Yuan’s words, and the rest of the Azure Dragon Clan began exchanging glances with each other. Eventually, Jiao Zhenhai spoke, “I am willing to negotiate, but I will only continue speaking if you, Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s Envoy, are alone. That means the rest of the Holy Dragon Clan must leave.” “That’s unreasonable!”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Did I hear you correctly? You want to visit the Azure Dragon Clan’s headquarters?” Jiao Zhenhai asked with a deep frown. “Yes,” Yuan replied with a nod. “And what exactly do you hope to accomplish?” Jiao Zhenhai pressed. “Our clan has lived there for tens of thousands of years. We’ve searched every corner, and nothing we’ve found has ever explained our situation or hinted at a solution.” “Who knows? I can’t tell you until I see it for myself. Maybe you missed something. What do you have to lose, anyway?”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After forming an airtight barrier of spiritual energy around himself—functioning like a self-contained air tank—Yuan stepped forward and followed Jiao Zhenhai into the depths of the Sea of Dragons. The waters of the Sea of Dragons were remarkably clear, allowing Yuan to see his surroundings with ease, even without the aid of any special techniques or artifacts. Once they had descended deep enough into the sea, one of the soldiers accompanying Jiao Zhenhai suddenly shifted into his true form—a massive, blue-scaled dragon whose presence seemed to ripple through the surrounding waters with raw majesty and power. “Hop on.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After passing through the underwater city, Yuan’s group continued their journey for several more hours, gliding deeper into the heart of the Azure Dragon Clan’s domain. Eventually, they arrived at their destination—a massive underwater courtyard residence that stretched across several miles of seafloor. Its grand architecture blended seamlessly with the surrounding ocean, featuring sweeping halls made of reinforced coral, shimmering crystal pillars, and roofs shaped like flowing waves. The entire structure radiated an aura of nobility and ancient power, befitting the status of a royal dragon clan. Yuan’s group landed right at the entrance, where a group of individuals from the Azure Dragon Clan stood in orderly formation, awaiting their arrival. “The Azure Dragon Clan welcomes the envoy of Dragon Goddess Yeyou and the Holy Dragon Emperor to our humble domain.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Where would you like to visit first, envoy of Dragon Goddess Yeyou?” Xuanbing asked. “That’s too long. Just call me Yuan.” “Then, Senior Yuan, where would you like to go?” Yuan pondered for a moment before speaking, “Let’s take a look around this place first since we’re already here.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Poison?!” Binglan suddenly burst out, her voice sharp with disbelief. “Are you saying poison is what caused the decline of our Azure Dragon Clan’s bloodline?! That’s outrageous!” Her eyes flared with fury as she stepped forward. “I’ve tolerated this farce only because it was my father’s wish—but I can no longer endure it! You’re nothing but a fraud!” “E-Elder sister… please, calm down,” Xuanbing said softly, stepping in with a concerned expression, trying to ease the rising tension. Yuan looked at her with raised eyebrows.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: As Yuan ventured deeper, the waters around him grew increasingly murky and dim. The clarity faded into a thick, shadowy haze, and a foul stench began to fill the area, seeping through even the protective barrier around him. It was the pungent, unmistakable smell of decay, as if he were swimming through water steeped in rot and corruption. “Heavens… It was nothing like this when we visited this place for the last time a hundred years ago…” The First Elder remarked with a disgusted expression on his face, even pinching his nose, trying to fight off the pungent smell. “The last time you visited this place was a hundred years ago?” Yuan asked to confirm.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: "Disciple Fei, Disciple Xuan! Wake up!" Elder Shan walked into the room shouting their name, startling them awake. "M-Master? What are you doing in our room?" Fei Yuyan asked her with a drowsy face, clearly still half asleep. "It's already morning! Aren't you going to meet with Master Zou today?" Elder Shan reminded her. Fei Yuyan's eyes widened when she noticed Yuan standing by the door, and she woke up almost instantly, "I-I am sorry! I overslept!"

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Why do you even care about such things when you’re about to die?” The First Elder sneered. “You should be worrying about your own life.” Yuan shrugged and replied, “Why bother worrying about something that’s out of my control? You’re a God Ascension Realm cultivator, while I am still a mortal. The outcome should be obvious. And if I am going to die, I want to at least know why.” “It’s simple. The reason you’re going to die is because you stuck your nose into something you shouldn’t have.” Yuan sighed, “What else am I supposed to do? I need the key you took from the Holy Dragon Clan. By the way, aren’t you afraid of offending Dragon Goddess Yeyou? In case you forgot, I am her envoy.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Don’t get ahead of yourself and think you’ve already defeated me! Although you managed to land a hit on me, this amount of damage is nothing—” “No, it’s over for you.” Yuan suddenly interrupted. “What are you—” Just as the First Elder opened his mouth to speak, he froze—his expression contorting in agony.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After the First Elder and the Third Elder left, Yuan returned to purifying the poison. A week later, the pitch-black taint that once clouded the waters had completely vanished. The sea now shimmered with clarity, indistinguishable from the untouched stretches around it—serene, pure, and free of corruption. Once he purified all of the poison, Yuan made his way back to the Azure Dragon Clan’s residence while taking the now-purified orb with him. However, he didn’t even make it halfway back to the Azure Dragon Clan’s living quarters before he encountered Jiao Zhenhai, Holy Dragon Emperor, and the others.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Eventually, the Azure Dragon Clan calmed down. “We’ll deal with the First Elder and the Third Elder later. For now… since you purified the poison, does that mean our problem has been fixed?” Jiao Zhenhai asked Yuan. He shook his head. “Hard to say. Since the Azure Dragon Clan has been exposed to the poison for thousands of years, it might have a permanent effect on your bloodline. However, it’s also possible for your bloodline to improve in future generations.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Thank you!” Xuanbing stood up once he regained enough energy and bowed to Yuan. “No problem.” They contacted Jiao Zhenhai and the others shortly after. “So? How are the results?” Jiao Zhenhai asked.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After Yuan offered the Immortal Monarch’s blood for the Tear of the Azure Heavens, Jiao Zhenhai began to seriously contemplate his offer. The Immortal Monarch’s blood would not only help the Azure Dragon Clan recover their weakened bloodline but might even strengthen it past their original purity. “H-How many drops of the Immortal Monarch’s blood do you possess?” Jiao Zhenhai asked in a hesitant tone. Yuan smiled and said, “Enough for you and all of your children.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: As if struck by a sudden nightmare, the Second Elder recoiled in horror. With a sharp gasp, he leapt backward instinctively. “W-What the hell are you?!” the Second Elder exclaimed, his voice riddled with fear. “How are you still alive?!” Before Yuan could even respond, the Second Elder spoke again, “It can’t be! That regenerative ability! You’re a demon!” “Hahaha!”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After putting away Number One Under Heaven, Yuan turned to look at Jiao Zhenhai, who was sitting on the floor with a dazed look on his face, and said, “I apologize for the destruction of this place.” Jiao Zhenhai looked around with a dumbfounded look on his face before responding, “I-It’s fine. As long as the treasures themselves aren’t damaged.” “Thank you. Now then, about what you were trying to tell me about our offer…” Jiao Zhenhai slowly stood up before speaking in a solemn tone, “I am willing to give you the Tear of the Azure Heavens for the Immortal Monarch’s blood, but I want one more thing from you.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Where’s Senior Envoy?” Jiao Xuanbing asked Jiao Zhenhai a month after they began preparing for war. “He’s in seclusion.” “Are you sure he’ll even be of any help? He’s only at the peak of Divine Emperor…” Jiao Binglan said, expressing her doubts. “And for the record, I still don’t believe he managed to defeat the First Elder.” Jiao Zhenhai looked at his foolish daughter and said, “I didn’t mention this, but it was he who killed the Second Elder. If it weren’t for him, I wouldn’t be standing here right now.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “WHAT?! YOU GAVE OUR FAMILY HEIRLOOM TO HIM?!” Binglan exclaimed with a horrified expression after hearing Jiao Zhenhai’s explanation. It wasn’t just Jiao Binglan. Jiao Xuanbing and the others were also just as shocked. None of them could’ve expected Jiao Zhenhai would be willing to part with their family heirloom. “I understand your feelings, but what use is our family heirloom if our clan is destroyed in the process of trying to protect it? Moreover, we will receive the Immortal Monarch’s blood! This is a blessing none of you can even begin to fathom!” Jiao Zhenhai said. “The Immortal Monarch’s blood?” Binglan and the others turned to look at Yuan with suspicious gazes.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: "I was told that if someone could play this zither, they would get to keep it. Is that true?" Yuan asked Senior Zou sometime later. Senior Zou laughed out loud after seeing Yuan's eyes that were filled with anticipation, and he nodded, "Yes, that's right." Yuan immediately smiled behind the black jade mask. "However, before I can give it to you, I want to know at least the face of the person I am giving this treasure to." Senior Zou suddenly narrowed his eyes at Yuan, seemingly suspicious of Yuan's identity.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: When Jiao Zhenhai felt his parents’ pressure bearing down on him, he quickly said, “W-Wait! He was the one who initiated the offer! Six drops of the Immortal Monarch’s blood are already a lot! If I asked for more, he would’ve likely refused!” His parents sat back down and withdrew their pressure after hearing this. “Do you think he’ll be willing to give two more drops of the Immortal Monarch’s blood—if he even has them, that is.” “Why are you asking me? You should ask him instead,” Jiao Zhenhai shrugged.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Y-You’re lying! There’s no way the Azure Dragon Clan gave away its family heirloom!” The Third Elder pointed his trembling hand at them and shouted, his voice filled with doubt and disbelief. “Who do you think you’re fooling, Jiao Zhenhai?! We know you better than anyone here! You wouldn’t dare to part with that treasure even at your deathbed!” The First Elder laughed out loud, not believing it for a second. Jiao Zhenhai sneered and said, “You don’t have to believe me, but you’ll end up fighting a pointless war at this rate.” “Pointless? Do you really think the Tear of the Azure Heavens is the only reason we’re here?” The Third Elder shook his head.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “A trick, huh? Well, I guess it may seem like that to you.” After putting away his weapon, Yuan slowly approached the First Elder. Seeing this, the First Elder hastily distanced himself from Yuan. A profound light flickered within Yuan’s eyes, and he spoke in a low but commanding tone, “Halt.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Noticing how fast their army was falling apart, the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan’s Immortals could no longer sit still and went after Yuan. “About time I have some worthy opponents!” Yuan laughed as he confronted a Bronze Immortal, and their Immortal Qi clashed. ‘What?! I’m being pushed back?!’ The Bronze Immortal was utterly stunned that he was being forced back by someone still in the Immortal Ascension Realm, a human cultivator who hadn’t even truly stepped into immortality.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: As the Immortals relentlessly unleashed their martial techniques on Yuan, he continued to ignore their attacks and struck whoever was the closest to him, reducing their numbers with terrifying speed and indifference, almost as if the difference in their cultivation meant nothing in his eyes. As time passed, Yuan’s prowess only continued to increase as the Dragon God’s Vengeance claimed more victims with a dragon bloodline. “Fuck! Why aren’t our attacks working on him?!” “Where did this monster come from?!”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 1975: Wielding Celestial Qi “H-Ho-How dare you… a mere mortal… MOCK ME?!” The God Ascension expert trembled in anger as he felt a sense of humiliation he hasn’t experienced for a long, long time. In his eyes, it was akin to an elephant being crippled by a single ant whom he could easily crush with a finger.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 1976: Mystic Sea Dragon Clan’s Reinforcement As the God Ascension expert turned to flee, Yuan immediately gave chase. However, being underwater—and with his opponent a native sea beast—Yuan quickly found himself outpaced, the distance between them widening with every passing second. Unable to close the distance, Yuan resorted to mockery, hurling taunts laced with scorn in hopes of provoking his opponent into a reckless, rage-fueled mistake. “You dare call yourself a God when you’re running away from a mortal like a frightened cat?!”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After hearing the Mystic Empress’ words, the Third Elder fell silent, no longer daring to utter another word. Instead, he glared at Yuan with a hateful gaze. ’It was all because of that bastard! If he hadn’t shown up, we wouldn’t be in this situation!’ The Third Elder blamed everything on Yuan, who showed up out of the blue and purified their poison. Unfortunately, the only thing he could do was complain about it.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “GET OUT OF MY WAY, YOU TRASH!” Mystic Empress’ aura exploded further. “At this rate, we won’t be able to block her for a single minute!” Jiao Longfei poured all of his Celestial Qi forward, but it quickly crumbled when Mystic Empress struck back. “Damn! When the hell did she become so powerful?!” Jiao Xuanji exclaimed as she struggled to keep up. “My treasure! My plans! My future! Ahhhhh!”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: While the Azure Dragon Clan worked hard to rebuild their destroyed underwater city, much of which was left in ruins by the invasion, Yuan focused on cleansing the lingering poison from the clan’s remaining children. Meanwhile, the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan did not stop moving at their peak speed until they escaped the water and returned to the surface. Once outside the water, Mystic Empress halted her movements and stared at the empty space before her in silence. The army behind didn’t dare to disturb her and patiently waited for her to snap out of it.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: "Who is your partner, Fairy Fei?" The worker asked her. Fei Yuyan pointed at Yuan who was still wearing his black jade mask and said, "My partner is a fellow disciple, Yuan." The worker turned to look at Yuan with raised eyebrows. 'Her partner's a man? What a lucky bastard,' The worker cursed at Yuan inwardly, feeling envious.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: The Mystic Empress became silent after learning of the identity of the entity who humiliated her. Everything finally made sense to her. If it was one of the Dragons of Origin, it was only natural that she was hopeless before such an entity. “If Dragon Goddess Yeyou has truly returned, this is huge news,” said one of the guests. The others nodded in agreement, “Who knows how everyone will react, especially the Great One.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: When Yuan arrived at the training ground, Xi Meili was engaged in a fierce spar with one of the young dragons. “What happened to her? She’s improved a lot since I last saw her. Even her cultivation has soared,” Yuan remarked as they watched from a distance. Xi Meili’s cultivation had somehow soared to the peak of Divine Ancestor in two short years, and her aura had grown so exponentially that Yuan almost didn’t recognize her at first. “She’s trained with our most experienced instructors nearly every single day for the last two years. It was already apparent from the very beginning that she had tremendous potential and talent, but we’ve underestimated how much talent was dwelling within her.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Though it was faint, the Lord could sense something trying to connect with the Dragon Spiral Mountain on Earth. “He did it! He activated the device from the other side!” Not daring to waste another moment, the Lord swiftly turned and rushed into the depths of his cave. There, waiting in solemn silence, stood a circular platform and an ancient stone tablet. Without hesitation, he drew forth a golden key and inserted it into the keyhole, its surface trembling faintly in response. However, unlike the teleportation device in the Seventh Heaven, the one on Earth barely emitted any glow due to a lack of spiritual energy.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Following the Holy Dragon Emperor’s firm refusal to comply, General Wang was left with no choice but to contact the Celestial Emperor for further instructions. The situation had grown too delicate, and any misstep could ignite a conflict that would affect not just the Celestial Emperor but the entire Nine Heavens. The Heaven’s Mandate certainly possessed the manpower to annihilate the Holy Dragon Clan if they so wished, but doing so would be no different than striking down a foreign diplomat protected by immunity, and they’d have to answer to the other Royal Dragon Clans. While the Celestial Emperor stood as the single most powerful and authoritative figure in the Nine Heavens, his reach was not absolute. There existed powers beyond even his command. Most notably, the Ten Royal Dragon Clans. As dragons, they stood at the very pinnacle of the beast world, alongside the Phoenix bloodline and a handful of other ancient bloodlines. And as royals among dragons, they possessed the ability to

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “How long have I been in here?” Yuan mumbled to himself as his body continued to travel through the space rift, feeling like it’d been ages since he entered the portal. However, in reality, it’d only been a few minutes since he entered. “Wait a second… this might be an opportunity to train my Power of the Void…” Realizing this, Yuan stopped thinking about the passage of time and focused solely on the void around him.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: When Yuan emerged from the space rift, for a brief moment, he thought the teleportation had failed—that he was still in the Seventh Heaven. The spiritual energy in the air was simply too dense, too rich, to belong to Earth. But he quickly realized that it was because the spiritual energy had flowed in from the Seventh Heaven. The moment the teleportation device connected the realms, the boundless spiritual energy from the Seventh Heaven flowed into Earth. Even though it was only a fraction, for a small world like Earth, even the tiniest trickle of spiritual energy from the Seventh Heaven was enough to blanket the entire planet, saturating every corner with spiritual energy it hadn’t experienced since the Immortal Monarch’s Era. The cultivators on Earth immediately sensed the shift. The moment the spiritual energy thickened around them, many dropped whatever they were doing and entered seclusion on the spot. Fearing it might vanish as suddenly as it appeared, they rushed to absorb as mu

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Young Master! Are you okay?!” Yu Ning and the others exclaimed, their voices filled with horror. “The Celestial Emperor’s Curse is trying to destroy my soul…” “What?! Then you should hurry up and destroy the curse using the Sacred Chalice of Purification!” Feng Yuxiang urged.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After leaving his home, Yuan immediately made his way back to the peak of the mountain. “Welcome back, Young Master.” The Lord, who was unconscious when he left, had woken up. “How are you feeling?” Yuan asked. “Thanks to your blood, my condition was able to stabilize. However, with my cultivation like this, I am no longer able to control the formation that protects this world. Fortunately, the spiritual energy from the Nine Heavens has temporarily solved this situation.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: The moment the Celestial Emperor sensed his curse destroyed by Yuan, a resolute glint flashed in his eyes. Without hesitation, he raised his hand, drawing forth his Celestial Qi. “Your Majesty! What are you doing?! You can’t use such a powerful technique without any warning!” A man bearing a unique white and gold robe hastily approached the Celestial Emperor and exclaimed after seeing this. “The results will be catastrophic, and the Celestial Council will be all over this!” “I am well aware,” he calmly responded. “However, I cannot let him return to the Nine Heavens. Even if I receive backlash for this, I am willing to bear the responsibility.” The man fell silent upon hearing the unwavering composure and clear finality in the Celestial Emperor’s tone. He was certain that unless someone forcefully stopped him, the Celestial Emperor had no intention of backing out.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 1989: The Appearance of a Cultivation God “A Cultivation God has appeared!” “What?! Impossible! Why would a Cultivation God intervene, especially when the Celestial Emperor is involved?” “Are we sure they are a Cultivation God? There are only a handful of them in the entire Nine Heavens, and I recognize everyone but this individual.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: 'His movements… His aura… This person is definitely a top zither expert! And judging by his clothes, he's… an Outer Court disciple from the Dragon Essence Temple?!' The veiled figure was greatly shocked by Yuan's uniform, silently wondering why he didn't join the Heavenly Melody Academy instead. 'With his abilities, he could easily become a Core Disciple at the Heavenly Melody Academy, even a high-ranking sect elder! Yet he decided to join the Dragon Essence Temple that doesn't have any zither experts? What reason could have possibly led him to such a foolish decision?! He's wasting his talents in that place!' This veiled individual did not believe the Dragon Essence Temple deserved an excellent zither expert like Yuan, even feeling a bit envious of them. The veiled individual suddenly had the urge to go up to Yuan and tell him to leave the Dragon Essence Temple and join the Heavenly Melody Academy instead. However, when they noticed Elder Xuan and Elder Shan's presence sitting not far

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 1990: Returning to the Nine Heavens “Th-The Cultivation God did it! She stopped the Celestial Emperor’s attack!” A collective gasp swept through the spectators as they drew in a sharp breath, stunned by the unforeseen turn of events. The moment the Divine Judgment shattered, the Celestial Emperor recoiled as if struck across the face, a searing pain radiating from it. He staggered, blood spilling from his lips, marking the second time in a single day that he had been wounded, a humiliation nearly as deep as the injury itself.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: As Yuan landed upon Dragon Spiral Mountain, a wave of shock and sorrow washed over him. The mountain was littered with thousands of corpses, many of whom were from the Holy Dragon Clan. The rest belonged to human cultivators wearing full armor. “Heaven’s Mandate…” Despite this being his first time encountering them, he instantly recognized them purely from their armor and its unique patterns. After all, he’d had plenty of encounters with them in his previous lives. “Did a war happen here or something? These corpses look fresh, so it happened only recently.” Liya remarked as she hovered slightly above the ground, not wanting to touch the corpses.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: “Please, don’t call it a sacrifice. We were merely defending our territory—our home,” the Holy Dragon Emperor said solemnly. Yuan’s expression turned awkward, guilt flickering in his eyes. “Even so… it’s a fact that the Celestial Emperor attacked because I activated the teleportation device. You wouldn’t have needed to do such a thing if I didn’t show up.” The Holy Dragon Emperor shook his head and said, “Your appearance here, as well as the teleportation device’s activation, is Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s will, and as the Holy Dragon Clan, it’s our duty to fulfill her will, regardless of its cost. I allowed anyone who didn’t want to die a chance to leave, but nobody took the offer. That’s all I have to say on this matter.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 1993: Ten Royal Dragon Clans “The audacity!” one of the representatives shouted, his voice trembling with fury. “Do you truly wish to wage war against the Ten Royal Dragon Clans, Celestial Emperor?!” The Celestial Council, frowning at the escalating tension, finally stepped in. “Let us all take a moment to calm ourselves,” one of the council members said firmly. “If the Ten Royal Dragon Clans were to truly wage war against His Majesty, the Celestial Emperor, it would result in countless deaths and destruction across the Nine Heavens.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 1994: Super Perfect Physique Tempering Sect “You weren’t there to see it, but it was quite the spectacle. I’m sure governments worldwide are making their way to the Dragon Spiral Mountain as we speak to investigate,” the Lord explained. “I see… where’s Miss Liya? I’m sure she’ll handle it.” The Lord showed a bittersweet smile and revealed, “Liya followed Yuan back to the Nine Heavens. She’s no longer with us.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 1995: Sparring With Kelan “By the way, who’s that pretty lady? Wait! Let me guess—she’s another lover—” “No, she’s a friend. I brought her here from my world,” Yuan hastily interrupted when he sensed Liya’s cold glare from behind. “What? She’s from the forgotten world?” Kelan looked at Liya with interest. “Why’d you bring her to the Nine Heavens?”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 1996: Sparring With Kelan(2) “This is my first time fighting in my true body, so give me everything you’ve got,” Yuan said as he beckoned Kelan. “Then… don’t mind if I do!” Kelan unleashed his cultivation, but he still kept it suppressed to the peak of Immortal Ascension.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 1997: Sparring With Kelan(3) “Fuck, I have never met or heard of anyone that’s even close to being as monstrous as you,” Kelan muttered after learning that Yuan was barely showing all of his potential but was still able to make a True Immortal feel helpless while only being a mortal. “You know, you should join… No, I’ll tell you about it later.” Kelan proceeded to take a deep breath before increasing his cultivation even further, stepping into the first level of God Ascension.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 1998: A Week With Meixiu Once Kelan left the sparring grounds, Yuan said, “We will have plenty of time to catch up, so I’m going to take some time to rest for now.” “Who are you going to stay with?” Chu Liuxiang asked. “Me or Meixiu?” He quickly replied, “I was going to take turns, so I would stay with one of you for a week, then switch, and repeat.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 1999: A Week With Chu Liuxiang “Relax, I won’t be going anywhere for the next week,” Yuan said with a smile after their kiss. Chu Liuxiang then dragged him to the bedroom. “I’ve been preparing for this moment,” she said.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: "Character status." Yuan activated the system command through his mind as he did with the talisman. Name: Yuan Cultivation: None Legacy: None

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After half an hour of running, Yuan and Xiao Hua could finally see the city walls. «You have discovered 'Spirit City'» "This place seems to be called Spirit City," said Yuan after reading the system notification. "Spirit City is one of the four Great Cities within the Eastern Continent, and it's ranked third in terms of influence." Xiao Hua quickly explained.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Yuan finished his song two minutes after the veiled lady stopped playing her zither, and he turned to look at her with his clear eyes afterward. "That was very fun. Thank you for playing with me," Yuan said with a smile behind his mask. "..." The veiled lady stared at Yuan's bright eyes in silence, seemingly pondering about something, and after a long moment of silence, she finally spoke for the first time in a very soothing voice, "How would you like to join the Heavenly Melody Academy? Whatever position you want, you can have."

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 2000: Grown Up “Look at Fairy Chu! She has her arms linked around that man!” The disciples quickly took notice of the intimate atmosphere around the two, which greatly shocked them. Chu Liuxiang and the other ladies within the Demon Sealing Faction had earned a reputation for themselves throughout the year for being unobtainable because even Core Disciples were shot down without any hesitation.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 2001: Yu Rou’s True Feelings “Brother!” Unable to contain herself, Yu Rou subconsciously threw herself into his arms, wrapping him in a tight embrace, years of longing and emotion pouring into that single, desperate gesture. “Y-Yuan?!” Bai Lianhua exclaimed in a shocked tone, her eyes wide with surprise.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 2002: Snowy’s Transformation “Is this blood from a magical beast or something?” Yu Rou asked as she uncorked the vial. A distinct scent immediately filled the air, one perceptible only to beasts. Snowy’s ears perked up, and her eyes widened with excitement, her instincts awakened by the unmistakable aroma. Like a beast in heat, Snowy pounced onto Yu Rou, who quickly shouted, “Ah! You’re going to make me drop it!”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 2003: Meeting Li Jinxi The following morning, Yu Rou slowly opened her eyes. “Brother…” she whispered, a warm smile blossoming on her face as she gazed at Yuan, peacefully asleep beside her. A sudden impulse stirred within her, and after a brief moment of hesitation, she leaned in and pressed a soft, gentle kiss on his lips.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 2004: Li Jinxi’s True Feelings “Come at me whenever you’re ready.” Li Jinxi said as she unleashed her full cultivation and as much Immortal Qi as she could muster without going out of control. However, despite her seemingly confident expression, Li Jinxi already knew that she wouldn’t be able to defeat Yuan, who could fight on par with God Ascension cultivators. In response, Yuan retrieved Number One Under Heaven and activated God of War’s Astral Arts from the get-go.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 2005: A New Addition to the Family After listening to Li Jinxi’s confession, Yuan smiled and said, “I’m flattered you feel that way about me, but I wasn’t doubting your feelings.” He continued, “After all, even though I have reincarnated many times, the feelings my incarnations experienced and their emotions are still mine, but not everyone will think like me. I just wanted to make sure that was what you really wanted.” “I am certain that’s what I want,” she responded with a serious face.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 2006: Faceless Sword Emperor “It’s been a while, Senior Bai.” Yuan greeted him with a warm smile. “Indeed, it has been a while. Last I heard from you, you were still in the Sixth Heaven. Where are you right now?” Senior Bai asked. “I’m currently in the Seventh Heaven, and I’ve been staying at Kelan’s sect for the last several months,” he said.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 2007: The Great One’s Appearance “Referral? From whom?” Yuan asked. “Only Sword Emperors and reputable swordmasters are allowed to give out referrals. Fortunately, we have someone in the faction who can give you a referral. I’ll contact them after this.” “Okay. What about the test?”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 2008: The Great One’s Appearance(2) Despite the Great One’s seemingly sudden appearance, the Azure Dragon Clan was not surprised at all. In fact, they’d anticipated his appearance when they learned that it was Dragon Goddess Yeyou who had stopped the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan’s invasion from Yuan. The Great One narrowed his golden eyes, his naturally arrogant gaze sweeping over the Azure Dragon Clan like he had absolute authority over them. “So, you’ve anticipated my arrival, have you?” he said, his voice calm yet imposing. “Then you must also know why I’m here. Let’s not waste time and tell me what I want to know. Is it true that Dragon Goddess Yeyou appeared here recently?”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 2009: The Great One’s Appearance(3) The Holy Dragon Emperor could only sigh inwardly after hearing the Great One’s declaration—that he’d defeat Dragon Goddess Yeyou. While it was true that Dragon Goddess Yeyou may not have been improving her strength since her disappearance because she’d lost her body, and her soul had split into several parts, nobody could say for certain that the Great One had surpassed her, but it was definitely a possibility. “Once I defeat her, I’ll finally obtain the one thing I’ve yearned but failed to claim in this life,” the Great One declared, his eyes burning with fervent determination.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: After leaving the park, Song Ling'er returned to the hotel she was staying at, but then she suddenly realized something that made her run back to the park. 'I forgot to ask that person for his name! What a blunder!' Song Ling'er cried inwardly when she realized that she'd forgotten to ask for Yuan's name due to her frustration at that moment. But alas, by the time she returned to the park, Yuan and the others were already long gone! 'Forget it. I'll ask Long Yijun when I visit the Dragon Essence Temple. Even if they try to hide him from me, I have already memorized his figure and hands so I'll recognize him at glance.'

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 2010: The Dragon Ancestor’s Unexpected Appearance Shortly after the second incident, news of the events at the Dragon Spiral Mountain spread through the Nine Heavens like wildfire. “You will not believe what just happened at the Dragon Spiral Mountain!” “Aren’t you a little late to the party? I heard about that literally a day after it happened several months ago.”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 2011: Referral Though his size paled in comparison to Kulas during his reign as the Giant Emperor, Kelan had reached a level of mastery that allowed him to amplify both his size and strength in just a few short months, something that required geniuses decades to achieve during the Primordial Era. “Come.” When Yuan beckoned him, Kelan charged like a sumo wrestler, each thunderous step shaking the ground beneath him. Just like before, Yuan met him head-on, unarmed and relying solely on his body.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 2012: Grand Sword Crater “When will you head to the Holy Sword Temple?” Xu Jiaqi asked Yuan shortly after giving him the sword-shaped badge. “I’ll have to figure out how to get there first,” he said. Though he’d been to the Holy Sword Temple before as Tian Xin, he couldn’t recall how to get there.

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 2013: Holy Sword Clan When Yuan saw the situation, he decided not to interrupt but to spectate instead. A few minutes later, the young man who’d caught the attention of the old man from the Holy Sword Clan opened his eyes and stood up, seemingly unaware of the elder’s presence. The old man cleared his throat loudly to announce his presence before speaking, “Young man, what’s your name?”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 2014: Holy Sword Clan(2) After a moment of silence, the old man cleared his throat and spoke in a forceful tone, “I must have misheard you just now. Can you repeat what you said?” “I said that I am not interested in joining the Holy Sword Clan,” Yuan calmly repeated, shocking everyone there for a second time. “Blasphemy! If you’re not interested in joining the Holy Sword Clan, why did you even bother with the metal plate?! Are you messing with me?!”

item.title: Cultivation Online
item.description: Chapter 2015: Holy Sword Clan(3) Unlike his predecessors, Tian Xin was unique in the sense that he was born with all of his previous life’s memories intact. This gave him an unfathomable advantage, allowing him to train with all of his previous knowledge since the day he was born. Because of this, Tian Xin was always considered a prodigy no matter where he went. Moreover, not only was Tian Xin born with all of his memories, but he also came from a renowned background, one that was known for creating many powerful swordmasters. One could consider Tian Xin being born with a diamond spoon in his mouth.
